mission.title: kubevirt: [debugging] [supportability] Make log verbosity changes more natural
mission.description: [debugging] [supportability] Make log verbosity changes more natural. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify kubevirt troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kubevirt deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt -n kubevirt --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kubevirt configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubevirt configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt
kubectl get configmap -n kubevirt -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubevirt
```
KubeVirt offers pretty nice support for dynamic log verbosity changing. However if one does not do that very often it always means looking up the documentation and editing the KubeVirt CR. This is cumbersome.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [debugging] [supportability] Make log verbosity changes…
steps.description:
This is not intended for being merged, but to give a reference to the
proof of concept.

In order to support Cloud Hypervisor inside KubeVirt, this pull request
modifies the existing virt-launcher component so that it manages Cloud
Hypervisor VMs instead of libvirt ones.

It adds support for the
```yaml
virtctl adm log-verbosity 3 --all
virtctl adm log-verbosity 3 --virt-handler --virt-controller
virtctl adm log-verbosity --reset
virtctl adm log-verbosity 2 --vmi <myvm>
```

steps.title: Upgrade kubevirt to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade kubevirt:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade kubevirt kubevirt/kubevirt --namespace kubevirt
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevirt
helm list -n kubevirt
```

steps.title: Confirm [debugging] [supportability] Make log verbosity… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevirt -n kubevirt --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kubevirt --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
This is not intended for being merged, but to give a reference to the
proof of concept.

In order to support Cloud Hypervisor inside KubeVirt, this pull request
modifies the existing virt-launcher component so that it manages Cloud
Hypervisor VMs instead of libvirt ones.
codeSnippets:
virtctl adm log-verbosity 3 --all
virtctl adm log-verbosity 3 --virt-handler --virt-controller
virtctl adm log-verbosity --reset
virtctl adm log-verbosity 2 --vmi <myvm>

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubevirt installed or the issue environment reproducible.
